purpose: Write-safety policy for the CAD OS Layer. Defines what each write_level is permitted to do, the preconditions a staged write must satisfy, and the hard prohibitions (raw command, original-DWG overwrite without approval). operations.v2.json write_level.default for every op MUST resolve to 'read' unless the op is explicitly a write/live family.

registry_binding._comment: config/operations.v2.json is bound to schemas/operation_registry.v2.schema.json and expresses write semantics in the v1 write_mode enum {read, write_copy, write_original, live_edit}. This policy uses the descriptive vocabulary {read, staged_write, write_original, live}. Each write_level below carries v1_write_mode_equivalent. Mapping: read=read, staged_write=write_copy, write_original=write_original, live=live_edit. Consumers should resolve an op's registry default_write_mode, translate via this mapping, then apply the matching write_level preconditions here.

read.description: Read-only DB/graph inspection. No mutation of any kind.

staged_write.description: Write to a STAGED COPY of the DWG only (never the original). Default for all write.* and apply.patch ops.
preconditions: copy: original DWG is copied into staging\golden\<stamp>\ before any write; original byte/mtime must remain unchanged.
backup: a pre-edit backup of the staged copy is retained alongside the working copy.
journal: every mutating operation is appended to an edit journal (op id, args, timestamp, target handle) before commit.

validation: post-write validation runs (e.g. entity-count / 3-way gate) and must pass before the staged result is accepted; on failure the staged copy is discarded and status=blocked/error is returned.
persist_via_qsave: if the staged copy is saved, persistence uses QSAVE, never Database.SaveAs.

write_original.description: Mutate the ORIGINAL DWG or the currently-open active document in place.
write_original.approval_token: explicit operator approval (CADOS write_original approval); packet-level approve P-NN where the original DWG path is a PROTECTED_PATH.
preconditions: explicit_approval: operator must explicitly authorize write_original for the specific drawing; default behavior NEVER escalates a staged_write to write_original automatically.
all staged_write preconditions (copy-as-backup, journal, validation) still apply; the pre-edit copy is the rollback artifact.
persist_via_qsave: persistence uses QSAVE, never Database.SaveAs (preserves the original-DWG safety contract).
report_changes: the operation must report exactly what was changed.
write_original.notes: No operation defaults to write_original. It is an opt-in escalation per the v1 capabilities write_original_policy.

live.description: Live runtime effects in an attended active-document session (reactors, overrules, jigs, live patch).
live.requires_approval: for live.apply_patch and any active-document mutation: yes; for ephemeral live.* runtime toggles (reactor/overrule enable/disable, jig probe): operator-initiated, no DWG mutation.
live.mutates_dwg: only when the live op writes the active document (then write_original rules apply)
preconditions: host: requires native_arx or full_autocad host (see host_matrix.v2.json); not headless-safe for observable live effects.
if the live op mutates the active document: all write_original preconditions apply (explicit approval + journal + QSAVE + report_changes).

raw_command_forbidden.rule: Arbitrary raw AutoCAD command / AutoLISP injection from job payloads is FORBIDDEN. Only the registered, enumerated operation ids in operations.v2.json may be dispatched.
raw_command_forbidden.rationale: Prevents route drift, fake extraction, and uncontrolled original-DWG mutation.

original_dwg_overwrite_without_approval.rule: Overwriting an original DWG without an explicit write_original approval is FORBIDDEN. Default write_mode=read; writes are staged_copy_only unless escalated.
original_dwg_overwrite_without_approval.rationale: Original CAD sources are READ-ONLY by default (CAD router invariant).

database_saveas_forbidden.rule: Database.SaveAs is FORBIDDEN for persistence; use QSAVE.

no_fake_success.rule: Unavailable/unimplemented operations MUST return explicit not_implemented/unavailable/blocked status. Claiming PASS for unrun or unsupported work is FORBIDDEN.
no_fake_success.rationale: no-fake-success invariant; mirrors the router guard that 'SDK_OPERATION_NOT_IMPLEMENTED' must not ship as a silent success stub.

secrets.rule: Never read or print .env/token/key/credential/.pem/.p12 in job payloads, journals, logs, or results.

write_lock.rule: The native ARIADNE_NATIVE_JOB and managed ARIADNE_CAD_JOB dispatchers take a document write lock before any write operation (LockDocument before runner.Run).
write_lock.read_ops_lock: Read-only ops also acquire the lock unconditionally at dispatcher entry; this is harmless (no-op in Core Console where there is no document manager) and is left as-is to match the existing dispatcher contract.

escalation_rules.read_to_staged: An inspect.*/validate.*/diff.* op NEVER escalates to a write. read is terminal for those ops.
escalation_rules.staged_to_original: staged_write -> write_original requires explicit operator approval per request; never automatic.
escalation_rules.default_resolution: If a job omits write_mode, the effective write_mode is the op's write_level.default from operations.v2.json, which is 'read' for all inspect/validate/diff/render/status ops, 'staged_write' for write.*/apply.patch, and 'live' for live.*/extend.* runtime ops. The global default_write_mode (read) governs any op whose write_level cannot be resolved.

blocked_operations._comment: Ops that are blocked by policy/host until their lane is wired (no-fake-success).
blocked_operations.render.layout: blocked: plot/publish render is host-bound (full_autocad); no headless render path implemented.
blocked_operations.live.apply_patch: blocked: live active-document edit requires full_autocad + explicit write_original approval; not implemented.

m04_registry_status_policy.catalogued_runtime: not runnable; known native catalog op without CAD OS handler
